attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Fix horizon feature editability in HTML doc

content.text: Now I have a clear picture. Let me create the branch and explain everything.

input.description: Create bug fix branch for horizon editability

content.content: Switched to a new branch 'fix/horizon-editable'

content.text:
Branch created. Here's the full breakdown:

---

## What Horizon is supposed to do

**Horizon** is a collapsible summary panel pinned below the header. Its purpose is to give you a high-level "radar" of your most important commitments across all lanes, without having to scroll the full calendar. It shows:

1. **Overdue bin** (per lane) — any item with a past `dateStr` that isn't marked done, shown in red. These act as a persistent "you still need to deal with this" reminder.

2. **Upcoming milestones / outcomes / events** (per lane) — only the three "high-signal" item types, sorted by days until due. Each shows a countdown label (`5d`, `Today`, `Past`). Clicking the text scrolls the main calendar to that day and highlights the item.

The **Scroll filter** mode (in settings tray) is a bonus: it dims horizon entries whose calendar dates haven't scrolled into view yet, so the panel tracks what you're currently looking at.

---

## The bug — two related editability problems

**1. Overdue items are completely read-only**
The overdue bin renders items with `isReadonly: true` (line 1709), which means text becomes a `<span>` — you can't click to edit. The type badge is also disabled (`data-action=""`). The tooltip text says *"Double-Tap to complete"* but the double-tap handler (line 2335) only fires inside `.track` — the overdue bin is excluded. Right-click *does* toggle done, but that's easy to miss and there's no edit path at all.

**2. Future hz-items have no inline edit**
The upcoming items in Horizon are rendered as custom `<li>` elements (lines 1738–1744). The text is a plain `<span data-action="seekToDate">` — clicking navigates to the date but there's no way to edit the text inline. Only the type badge (`cycleType`) is interactive. To edit the text you have to navigate to the calendar row and edit it there.

---

## What needs fixing

- Replace the `index-text` span in future hz-items with an editable `<input data-action="editTask">` (same as normal items use), so text can be changed directly in the panel.
- Extend the double-tap `toggleDone` handler to also cover `.hz-item` elements (currently it only covers `.track` and `.proj-task-list`).
- Optionally: make overdue clones editable (or at minimum make right-click-to-done more discoverable with a visible button).

Want me to proceed with implementing these fixes?

content.text: [Request interrupted by user]

message.content: lets review where we are and where we can set off from

content.text:
Sure. Here's where things stand:

---

## What's done

**App fixes (all 5 changes applied to `Swimlane_App.html` on branch `fix/horizon-editable`):**

1. **CSS** — `.index-text` now works as an `<input>` (transparent, borderless, inherits font). Added `.hz-nav` hover-reveal navigation arrow.
2. **`updateTaskText`** — patches `input.value` instead of `span.textContent` for horizon items; removed the `horizonChanged` emit that was causing focus loss while typing.
3. **`horizonData()`** — future hz-items now render text as `<input class="index-text action-input" data-action="editTask">` + a separate `↗` nav button for date seeking.
4. **`editTask` handler** — extended `closest('.item')` to `closest('.item') || closest('.hz-item')` so the input event resolves the item ID correctly.
5. **`touchend` handler** — guard now also allows `.overdue-bin` and `.hz-item` ancestry, so double-tap-to-complete works in the horizon panel.

The browser check passes cleanly (no JS errors, app boots fine).

---

## What's broken — the test infrastructure

Tests T8–T11 are written and structurally correct but the **state seeding** fails. The diagnostic shows:

```
verify before reload → lanes: ["test-lane"], items: 1  ✓ (write worked)
verify after reload  → lanes: ["l_1","l_2","l_3"], items: 1  ✗ (lanes reverted)
```

The IDB write succeeds, but on `page.reload()` the app's **initScript** runs again and the app **overwrites IDB** during bootstrap — specifically `closeOnboarding` was called in T3, which called `Actions.mutate` → `debouncedSave`, and the saved 3-lane default state is somehow re-emerging. The items survived (1 item stays) but lanes revert to the default `l_1/l_2/l_3` — which means the app is doing a **partial merge** of default state + IDB items.

The root cause is almost certainly in `hydrate()` or the initial `state` declaration — the item with `laneId: 'test-lane'` survives the filter (it has all required fields) but the lane itself isn't in the loaded state, so the app falls back to default lanes.

---

## The fix for seeding

Two options:

**A (simple)** — Seed the item with a real default lane ID (`l_1`) instead of `test-lane`, so it references a lane the app actually renders. No infra changes needed.

**B (proper)** — Fix `seededPage` so the reload doesn't clobber the lanes — likely by also writing `swimlane_onboarded` before reload so no onboarding action triggers a save.

Option A is far simpler and the tests still verify the right behavior. Want me to go with A and clean up all the debug logging, then get the full suite green?
